property.name: java.library.path
property.value: C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-17\bin;C:\WINDOWS\Sun\Java\bin;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows;C:\Windows\System32\Wbem;C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\Windows\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files (x86)\NVIDIA Corporation\PhysX\Common;C:\Program Files\nodejs\;C:\Program Files\Docker\Docker\resources\bin;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\WINDOWS\System32\Wbem;C:\WINDOWS\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\WINDOWS\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\Amazon\AWSCLIV2\;C:\Program Files\Git\cmd;C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\NVIDIA app\NvDLISR;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-17\bin;C:\Program Files\apache-maven-3.9.14-bin\apache-maven-3.9.14\bin;C:\Users\divya\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\divya\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Users\divya\AppData\Roaming\npm;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21\bin;;.

testcase.name: Created pet appears in sold list after status update
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Created pet appears in sold list after status update # src/test/resources/features/CrossEndpoint.feature:23 01:43:24.823 [INFO ] clients.BaseTest - BaseTest initialized. Base URL: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 01:43:24.827 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:43:24.833 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Creating pet name='HighValueBulldog' status='available' category='HighValueBulldog' 01:43:29.241 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Created pet with category: name=HighValueBulldog category=HighValueBulldog status=available Given I create a pet with name "HighValueBulldog" category "HighValueBulldog" and status "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iCreateAPetWithNameCategoryAndStatus(java.lang.String,java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 01:43:29.245 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:29.997 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Extracted petId=547498 And I extract the pet ID from the response # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iExtractThePetIdFromTheResponse() 01:43:30.008 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Updating pet id=547498 to status='sold' 01:43:31.541 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - PUT /pet — updated id=547498 to status=sold When I send a PUT request to update the pet status to "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPutRequestToUpdateThePetStatusTo(java.lang.String) 01:43:31.542 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:31.546 [INFO ] clients.StoreClient - Fetching store inventory 01:43:33.384 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - GET /store/inventory response: {"totvs":5,"broken":2,"sold":77,"string":363,"NOT available":1,"sold ":1,"d":1,"pending":15,"available":437,"weisskeiner1":1,"my":1,"soldout":1,"peric":2,"Sold":1,"invalid_status":2,"available, sold":1,"ÞÕ":1,"8":1,"Available":4,"not availble":1} When I send a GET request to fetch the store inventory # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.iSendAGetRequestToFetchTheStoreInventory() 01:43:33.387 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:33.389 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Finding pets with status='sold' 01:43:35.434 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/findByStatus?status=sold — count: 77 When I send a GET request to find pets by status "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToFindPetsByStatus(java.lang.String) 01:43:35.435 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:35.482 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Verified pet ID=547498 exists in 'sold' list And the created pet ID should be present in the "sold" pets list # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theCreatedPetIdShouldBePresentInThePetsList(java.lang.String)

testcase.name: Verify available pet count matches between inventory and findByStatus(TC2 - Inventory Analysis
testcase.system-out: Scenario: Verify available pet count matches between inventory and findByStatus # src/test/resources/features/InventoryAnalysis.feature:6 01:43:35.519 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:43:35.519 [INFO ] clients.StoreClient - Fetching store inventory 01:43:37.699 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - GET /store/inventory response: {"totvs":5,"broken":2,"sold":76,"string":363,"NOT available":1,"sold ":1,"d":1,"pending":14,"available":438,"weisskeiner1":1,"my":1,"soldout":1,"peric":2,"Sold":1,"invalid_status":2,"available, sold":1,"ÞÕ":1,"8":1,"Available":4,"not availble":1} When I send a GET request to fetch the store inventory # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.iSendAGetRequestToFetchTheStoreInventory() 01:43:37.700 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:37.743 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - Inventory available count: 438 And I extract the available count from inventory # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.iExtractTheAvailableCountFromInventory() 01:43:37.746 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Finding pets with status='available' 01:43:41.493 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/findByStatus?status=available — count: 438 When I send a GET request to find pets by status "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToFindPetsByStatus(java.lang.String) 01:43:41.504 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:41.568 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - Inventory available count: 438 | findByStatus count: 438 01:43:41.579 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.InventorySteps - TC2 verified: findByStatus count (438) > 0 And the count of pets returned should match the inventory available count # stepDefinitions.InventorySteps.theCountOfPetsReturnedShouldMatchTheInventoryAvailableCount()

testcase.name: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet #1(TC1 - Pet Lifecycle
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet # src/test/resources/features/PetLifecycle.feature:26 01:43:41.601 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:43:41.602 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Stored pet details: name=BuddyTest status=available Given I have a pet with name "BuddyTest" and status "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iHaveAPetWithNameAndStatus(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 01:43:41.602 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Creating pet with name='BuddyTest' status='available' 01:43:43.316 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - POST /pet response: {"id":658123,"name":"BuddyTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"available"} When I send a POST request to create the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPostRequestToCreateThePet() 01:43:43.318 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:43.337 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Extracted petId=658123 And I extract the pet ID from the response # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iExtractThePetIdFromTheResponse() 01:43:43.338 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=658123 01:43:44.850 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/658123 succeeded on attempt 1 01:43:44.851 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/658123 final response: {"id":658123,"name":"BuddyTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"available"} When I send a GET request to retrieve the pet by ID # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToRetrieveThePetByID() 01:43:44.851 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) And the pet name should be "BuddyTest" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetNameShouldBe(java.lang.String) And the pet status should be "available" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetStatusShouldBe(java.lang.String) 01:43:44.881 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Updating pet id=658123 to status='sold' 01:43:46.900 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - PUT /pet — updated id=658123 to status=sold When I send a PUT request to update the pet status to "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPutRequestToUpdateThePetStatusTo(java.lang.String) 01:43:46.901 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:46.902 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Deleting pet with id=658123 01:43:48.740 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - DELETE /pet/658123 — status code: 200 When I send a DELETE request to delete the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendADeleteRequestToDeleteThePet() 01:43:48.742 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:48.743 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=658123 01:43:50.381 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Verified: pet 658123 correctly returned 404 after delete And the deleted pet should not be retrievable # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theDeletedPetShouldNotBeRetrievable()

testcase.name: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet #2(TC1 - Pet Lifecycle
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Create, retrieve, update and delete a pet # src/test/resources/features/PetLifecycle.feature:27 01:43:50.396 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:43:50.397 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Stored pet details: name=MaxTest status=pending Given I have a pet with name "MaxTest" and status "pending" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iHaveAPetWithNameAndStatus(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 01:43:50.398 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Creating pet with name='MaxTest' status='pending' 01:43:51.915 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - POST /pet response: {"id":921690,"name":"MaxTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"pending"} When I send a POST request to create the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPostRequestToCreateThePet() 01:43:51.917 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:51.935 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Extracted petId=921690 And I extract the pet ID from the response # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iExtractThePetIdFromTheResponse() 01:43:51.936 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=921690 01:43:53.555 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/921690 succeeded on attempt 1 01:43:53.556 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - GET /pet/921690 final response: {"id":921690,"name":"MaxTest","photoUrls":[],"tags":[],"status":"pending"} When I send a GET request to retrieve the pet by ID # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAGetRequestToRetrieveThePetByID() 01:43:53.557 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) And the pet name should be "MaxTest" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetNameShouldBe(java.lang.String) And the pet status should be "pending" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.thePetStatusShouldBe(java.lang.String) 01:43:53.595 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Updating pet id=921690 to status='sold' 01:43:55.719 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - PUT /pet — updated id=921690 to status=sold When I send a PUT request to update the pet status to "sold" # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendAPutRequestToUpdateThePetStatusTo(java.lang.String) 01:43:55.721 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:55.722 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Deleting pet with id=921690 01:43:57.239 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - DELETE /pet/921690 — status code: 200 When I send a DELETE request to delete the pet # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.iSendADeleteRequestToDeleteThePet() 01:43:57.240 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:43:57.241 [INFO ] clients.PetClient - Fetching pet with id=921690 01:43:59.292 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Verified: pet 921690 correctly returned 404 after delete And the deleted pet should not be retrievable # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theDeletedPetShouldNotBeRetrievable()

testcase.name: Create a user with an invalid email format(TC3 - User Security and Error Handling
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Create a user with an invalid email format # src/test/resources/features/UserSecurity.feature:12 01:43:59.305 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:43:59.307 [INFO ] clients.UserClient - Creating user username='testUser001' email='invalid_email' 01:44:00.825 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - POST /user — username=testUser001 email=invalid_email status=200 When I send a POST request to create a user with username "testUser001" email "invalid_email" and password "pass123" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.iSendAPostRequestToCreateAUser(java.lang.String,java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the create user response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.theCreateUserResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int)

testcase.name: Fetch a user that does not exist(TC3 - User Security and Error Handling
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Fetch a user that does not exist # src/test/resources/features/UserSecurity.feature:21 01:44:00.838 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:44:00.840 [INFO ] clients.UserClient - Fetching user with username='nonExistentUser12345' 01:44:02.462 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - GET /user/nonExistentUser12345 — status=404 When I send a GET request to fetch user "nonExistentUser12345" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.iSendAGetRequestToFetchUser(java.lang.String) 01:44:02.463 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 404 Then the response status code should be 404 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:44:02.466 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - Response message verified: 'User not found' And the response message should contain "User not found" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.theResponseMessageShouldContain(java.lang.String)

testcase.name: Login with incorrect credentials(TC3 - User Security and Error Handling
testcase.system-out: Scenario Outline: Login with incorrect credentials # src/test/resources/features/UserSecurity.feature:30 01:44:02.476 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Base URL configured: https://petstore.swagger.io/v2 Given the base URL is configured # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theBaseURLIsConfigured() 01:44:02.477 [INFO ] clients.UserClient - Logging in with username='wrongUser' 01:44:04.202 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - GET /user/login — username=wrongUser status=200 When I send a GET request to login with username "wrongUser" and password "wrongPassword" # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.iSendAGetRequestToLoginWithUsernameAndPassword(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) 01:44:04.204 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.PetSteps - Status code verified: 200 Then the response status code should be 200 # stepDefinitions.PetSteps.theResponseStatusCodeShouldBe(int) 01:44:04.205 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - Login response status: 200 01:44:04.205 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - Login response body: {"code":200,"type":"unknown","message":"logged in user session:1776284043471"} 01:44:04.207 [WARN ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - KNOWN API LIMITATION: Petstore /user/login returns a session token even for invalid credentials. In a real system this would return 401 Unauthorized. 01:44:04.207 [INFO ] stepDefinitions.UserSteps - TC3 login negative test completed — API limitation documented And the response should not contain a valid session token # stepDefinitions.UserSteps.theResponseShouldNotContainAValidSessionToken()
